title: IPFS API Vocabulary
description: Key terms and concepts used in IPFS APIs

CID.label: Content Identifier
CID.definition: A self-describing content-addressed identifier used to point to data stored in IPFS. CIDs are based on the content's cryptographic hash.

DAG.label: Directed Acyclic Graph
DAG.definition: The data structure used by IPFS to represent files and directories. DAG nodes are linked by CIDs.

IPNS.label: InterPlanetary Name System
IPNS.definition: A mutable addressing system built on top of IPFS that allows pointing a name to a CID that can be updated over time.

Pin.label: Pin
Pin.definition: An operation that prevents IPFS garbage collection from removing a block or DAG from local storage.

Peer.label: Peer
Peer.definition: A node in the IPFS network identified by a Peer ID (multihash of its public key).

Multiaddr.label: Multiaddress
Multiaddr.definition: A self-describing network address format used in IPFS and libp2p. Example: /ip4/127.0.0.1/tcp/4001/p2p/QmPeer...

Swarm.label: Swarm
Swarm.definition: The network layer of IPFS responsible for peer connections and data transfer.

CAR.label: Content Addressable aRchive
CAR.definition: A format for packaging IPFS DAG data into a single file, used for export and import of content.

MFS.label: Mutable File System
MFS.definition: A virtual filesystem API in IPFS that allows working with files and directories as if IPFS had a traditional mutable filesystem.

Bitswap.label: Bitswap
Bitswap.definition: The data trading module in IPFS, responsible for requesting and sending blocks to/from other peers.

DHT.label: Distributed Hash Table
DHT.definition: The distributed routing table used by IPFS to discover content providers and peer addresses.

Gateway.label: IPFS Gateway
Gateway.definition: An HTTP server that provides access to IPFS content over standard HTTP, enabling access without running a local IPFS node.

PinningService.label: Pinning Service
PinningService.definition: A remote service that pins IPFS content on behalf of users, keeping it accessible on the network.

RequestID.label: Request ID
RequestID.definition: A unique identifier for a pin request in the IPFS Pinning Service API. Format: UUID v4.
